title: apargia danubialis
intro: Apargia danubialis, also known as Danube fleabane, is a perennial herb in the daisy family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic to help reduce fluid retention and alleviate symptoms of arthritis, fever, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. Apargia danubialis is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
